summary: Batch PR for scratch-submission matches, resolving #1787, #1791, #1792, and #1793. The largest structural change renamed the old unit src/melee/un/un_2FC98.c to src/melee/un/un_3028.c, updated splits/configure entries, and moved DevText/un_80302xxx declarations out of un_2FC9.h into a new un_3028.h consumed by many debug/game-mode/stage files. It also added several concrete toy.c matches and layout updates, introduced the Sheik chain item header/source separation, and made small signature/include fixes across db, gm, gr, ftSeak, lb, and un files. The decomp.dev report showed +480 bytes matched overall, 44 new matches and 10 improvements, with most apparent broken matches caused by the unit rename from un_2FC98 to un_3028.
what_changed: Renamed the split/configure unit from melee/un/un_2FC98.c to melee/un/un_3028.c, matching the text start address 0x80302834.
Created src/melee/un/un_3028.h and moved the DevText_Create/DevText_Print/DevText_SetCursorXY/etc. and un_80302xxx through un_80304xxx prototypes out of un_2FC9.h.

Updated many users of DevText/un_3028 symbols to include un/un_3028.h, including dbanim, dbbonus, dbcamera, dbcpu, dbitem, dbsound, gm_16F1, gm_1A4C, gm_1BF9, gmmovieend, gmstaffroll, ground, lbcardgame, toy, and un_2FC95/un_2FC96.
Adjusted symbols.txt for gm_803DB25C size from 0x6C to 0x68.
Added matched implementations in toy.c for un_80306B18, un_80306BB8, and un_803122D0_OnInit, plus more precise prototypes in toy.h.
Expanded struct Toy in ty/types.h with x4, x8, x194, and x19A fields and removed the old sizeof 0x58 static assertion because the scratch match required a larger layout.
Introduced src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c stubs/comments and expanded itseakchain.h with Sheik chain item prototypes; moved those prototypes out of generic it_27CF.h.

Changed ftSeak code to include it/items/itseakchain.h instead of it/items/it_27CF.h and forward-declared itChainSegment in ftSeak/forward.h while defining struct itChainSegment without the typedef form in ftSeak/types.h.
Fixed gmstaffroll gm_801AA664 to return gm_80164840(arg0) instead of dropping the return value.
Performed include-order and formatting adjustments in several files, often to satisfy declaration dependencies after the header split.
smart_moves: The unit rename to un_3028 aligns the source filename, split entry, configure object, and new header guard with the actual address range, making bot-reported match movement understandable as a rename rather than real regressions.
Separating un_3028.h from un_2FC9.h reduced an overgrown header and allowed DevText/debug-display declarations to be included explicitly by users.
Moving Sheik chain item declarations from it_27CF.h into itseakchain.h made ftSeak_SpecialS depend on the item module it actually calls rather than an unrelated generic item header.
Forward-declaring itChainSegment in ftSeak/forward.h avoided forcing the full ftSeak/types.h layout into every chain item user.
The toy.c matches were supported by a targeted Toy struct expansion rather than leaving accesses as opaque pointer arithmetic.
The bot report was interpreted in context: it listed 40 broken matches in un_2FC98 and 44 new matches in un_3028 because the object was renamed, while the net project movement was small but positive.
decomp_lessons: When a nonmatching unit has a name based on a previous boundary guess, rename it to the true address-based unit and update splits.txt plus configure.py together; otherwise match reports and include paths become misleading.
Do not keep prototypes for a newly separated address range in the previous unsplit header; create a dedicated header and update all direct users.
Header cleanup can be a prerequisite for matches: moving declarations to the right module and adding forward declarations can unblock source files without introducing circular includes.
For item/fighter cross-dependencies, prefer a narrow item-specific header such as itseakchain.h over pulling declarations from a broad address-bucket header.
Small semantic fixes, such as returning the result of a bool-returning call, can be necessary matching fixes even when the diff is tiny.
Decomp.dev rename churn should be read carefully: broken matches in the old unit paired with new matches in the renamed unit are not necessarily true regressions.
naming_conventions: Address-range unit names use the start address, e.g. un_3028 for code beginning at 0x80302834.
New headers for address-range units use matching guards such as GALE01_302834.
Unidentified functions retained address-style names such as un_80306B18, un_80306BB8, un_803122D0_OnInit, and fn_80303EF4.
Item-specific module naming used itseakchain.c/itseakchain.h for Sheik chain behavior rather than leaving declarations in it_27CF.h.
Opaque or partially known struct fields stayed offset-based, e.g. Toy.x4, Toy.x8, Toy.x194, Toy.x19A.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: toy.c un_80306B18 matched by expressing HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, Toy field stores, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 creation, and HSD_GObj_80390CD4 in structured C.
toy.c un_80306BB8 matched a two-path update: if Toy user data exists, decrement x8 and animate until cleanup; otherwise reset/animate the JObj based on lb_8000B09C.

un_803122D0_OnInit used an expanded Toy-backed buffer at un_804A26B8, memzero on x194 for 0x25A bytes, mode checks via gm_8016B498/gm_801A4310, a conditional target pointer, and a bitwise OR of 4 on the selected u16.
Matching required exact struct offsets in Toy: x4 at +0x4, x8 at +0x8, x194 at +0x194, and x19A at +0x19A.
gm_801AA664 matching required returning the bool result of gm_80164840(arg0).
dbcamera kept matching-sensitive helpers such as cstick_threshold and fn_80227EB0_dummy_inline, while include additions supplied un_3028/DevText declarations.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 38.02%, +0.01%, +480 bytes.
The bot listed 44 new matches, mostly in main/melee/un/un_3028 plus toy functions, and 40 broken matches in main/melee/un/un_2FC98 caused by the file/unit rename.
The bot also reported 10 improvements in unmatched un_3028 functions and 11 regressions in unmatched functions, including a small -4 byte regression in main/melee/ty/tyfigupon tyFigupon_80314C5C.
follow_up_queries: Inspect issues #1787, #1791, #1792, and #1793 for the original scratch submissions and any attribution/details not included in this PR slice.
Verify whether the duplicate prototypes for it_802BAEEC and it_802BAF0C in itseakchain.h were intentional or later cleaned up.
Check follow-up PRs touching un_3028.h or un_2FC9.h for additional header split cleanup.
Investigate the small tyfigupon regression reported by the bot to see whether it was rename noise or a real unmatched-function drift.
